candidate_problems.text: What precise properties must a minimal generative substrate have to yield both emergent Lorentzian spacetime geometry and quantum phenomena such as interference, entanglement, and Born-rule statistics, without simply re-encoding GR or QM by hand?

How can computational irreducibility be made scientifically sharp in fundamental physics—distinguished from mere practical intractability—and what empirical signatures would show that no compact closed-form unification is available even in principle?

By what mechanism do stable, compressive higher-level laws emerge from an irreducible substrate, so that GR-like smooth geometry, QM-like state evolution, and classical definite histories are all reliable coarse-grained descriptions in their respective regimes?
